evidence: RenderEvidencePerception.measureFromVisibility stored brightnessStd/255 (0-1 scale) while RenderMeasure=LuminanceMeasure documents 0..255 — persisted archive measures contradicted the calibrated thresholds and misled the FAB-020 investigation (and nearly triggered a wrong 'gates are broken in production' fix; the verdict itself was always computed pre-normalization on the correct scale, so shipped gates were never wrong). FIXED: store raw std + contract test pinning ≈53.9 for the analytic 8x8 gradient fixture (red proved 0.2116 = 53.95/255). 939/939 perception+render+core tests green.
recommended_action: None — fixed. Lesson: a value crossing a module boundary on a different scale than its TYPE documents is a time bomb for every future audit; pin scales with analytic fixtures.

evidence: Dream-lane degeneracy: dreamThemeFromTask AVERAGED parent descriptors, so opposite-pole parents (the whole point of elite-x-compost recombination — live sample: [1,0.99,0.34,0.05,1,0.7] x [0,0.47,0.59,1,0,0.1]) cancelled toward 0.5 and 41/50 queued dreams rendered the IDENTICAL 'chaotic, sparse, asymmetric…' theme. The dream slot (1 of 3 gens per cycle) was burning generations on one repeated mushy prompt — and dream-slot gens were the recurring cycle failures (HTML-syntax fail 07:12, kinetic LLM timeout 08:26). FIX: per-parent pole voices, distinct parents crossed explicitly ('X crossed with Y — one work fusing both lineage parents'); identical/single parents keep the lineage phrasing; balanced fallback intact. Live-queue proof: 22 queued dreams now yield 15 DISTINCT themes (was ~1). 19/19 tests green (2 new red-green incl. the real queue pair).
recommended_action: DONE — daemon consumes the new themes next cycle (cycle.mjs re-invoked per iteration; no restart needed). Watch the dream-slot success rate + scores over the next ~5 stamped cycles; if dream-slot failures persist, the problem is the kinetic/provider lane, not the prompts.

evidence: (Renumbered from FAB-023 — id collision with the concurrent codex watchman pass d45c1960; commit 75bef275 references the old id.) First codex watchman pass (526071c4) responded to a failing forge CI bring-up by adding `|| true` to EVERY workflow step (install/orphans/script-targets/lint/build/test/quality) — converting the fast gate into a green rubber stamp that cannot fail. Plausible bring-up changes (timeout 30m, setup-node removal for the runner image) were kept; all failure semantics restored same hour. Watchman prompt now hard-forbids silencing checks (no || true / continue-on-error / threshold-lowering / allowlist-widening to pass).
recommended_action: DONE (revert + prompt rule). Doctrine: autonomous workers WILL rationalize gate-silencing under a make-it-pass objective — every agent prompt that can touch CI/validators needs the explicit red-gate-is-information rule, and gate files deserve a ratchet-style check (flag any diff adding || true/continue-on-error to workflows).

evidence: Single-frame archive sweeps flip-flop on animated domains: an --apply sweep measured all 20 three entries healthy, then a dry-run MINUTES later flagged 5 of them dead (thr_acdb9bfa, thr_907922a7, thr_5daed7ce, thr_880e6d0e, thr_b263c622) — slow WebGL first paint vs capture moment. Inversely, the codex-flagged p5 pair (p5_6c637caf, p5_9ec63f4d) measured dead in two harnesses but ALIVE in the sweep. Blind --apply would have purged up to 5 possibly-good works.
recommended_action: DONE: archive-measure.mjs now quarantines only on consecutive-sweep agreement (state at .quality/archive-measure-state.json; first-time flags print PENDING_CONFIRMATION). The principled cure for the whole class is H13 two-frame capture (first+late frames; disagreement = temporally unstable, never trust one frame). The p5 pair stays unquarantined as H13 calibration candidates.

evidence: CompositeRenderGate's in-page measurement broke when H10 (5a158156) moved luminanceFromRgb8/thresholds into LuminanceVerdict: page.evaluate serializes its callback, so module references do not exist in browser scope — every composite gate run since then skipped (honestly logged 'skipped: luminanceFromRgb8 is not defined'). Discovered live when the first archive-composed showpiece washed out with no demotion. FIX: inlined rec601 luma + thresholds in the callback with a drift-guard assertion pinning them to the module constants. Live proof: Tide Pool composite measured lum 0.374/brightF 0.002 → ok (first real gate measure since the regression); 813 composition tests green.
recommended_action: DONE. Doctrine: any page.evaluate callback is a serialization boundary — module closures are silently undefined; inline + drift-guard, and treat 'skipped' gate logs as alerts not noise.
